assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an accessible email inbox.
No SAP.com identity, BTP global account, subaccount, runtime, or service instance exists yet.
The developer has not preselected trial versus enterprise free-tier, Cloud Foundry versus Kyma, or a specific BTP service.

developer_goal: Obtain SAP BTP access and reach the earliest meaningful first-success outcome for a chosen account model, runtime, and service or workload.

first_success.outcome: For the personal BTP Trial candidate: reach a provisioned trial subaccount where Service Marketplace service tiles and the filter bar are visible.
first_success.observable_signal: Service Marketplace shows available service tiles and a filter bar in the trial cockpit, when trial marketplace readiness is accepted as the terminal.

route_selection.surface: SAP BTP Trial and enterprise global-account onboarding
route_selection.rule: Reconstruct trial and enterprise candidates, then select only after account model, runtime, and first-success boundary are fixed. Equal consequential routes remain unresolved.

candidates.name: Personal SAP BTP Trial to cockpit subaccount and Service Marketplace visibility
candidates.reason: Documented self-service path for individuals, but trial policy forbids production or team development and is not the enterprise entitlement path.

candidates.name: Enterprise or free-tier global account via customer/partner contract
candidates.reason: Different eligibility, admin handoffs, entitlements, and lifecycle from personal trial.

candidates.name: Trial plus Cloud Foundry sample app deploy or Business Application Studio first workload
candidates.reason: Later getting-started outcomes that add runtime, tooling, and deployment actions beyond marketplace readiness.

required_path.action: Register an SAP.com identity if one does not already exist, then activate it from the emailed link.

required_fields.label: SAP.com registration fields
required_fields.notes: Tutorial says fill required fields but does not enumerate them.

required_path.observable_result: The SAP identity is activated and can sign in.

required_path.action: Start SAP BTP Trial with Try Now and complete the trial signup instructions.
required_path.observable_result: A trial global account is available in the BTP cockpit.

required_path.action: Create or open the trial subaccount in a chosen region and continue into the subaccount.
required_path.observable_result: The trial subaccount tile opens with environment and space information.

required_path.action: Open Services > Service Marketplace and confirm service tiles are listed.
required_path.observable_result: Service tiles and the marketplace filter bar are visible.

prerequisites.description: An SAP identity is required before BTP Trial or enterprise global-account access.
Trial use is limited to personal, non-productive exploration.

external_gates.description: New sap.com registration requires email activation before trial login.
Enterprise or free-tier commercial accounts require customer or partner entitlement rather than personal trial eligibility.

unavoidable_waits.description: SAP must deliver the account activation email for new registrations.
Trial subaccount provisioning runs before the Continue navigation into the new account.

platform_outcomes.description: SAP provisions a trial global account and subaccount after Try Now and region creation.
Service Marketplace lists services available to the trial environments.

excluded.item: Reading BTP conceptual documentation beyond getting-started and the trial tutorial
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a product action.

excluded.item: Deploying a Cloud Foundry sample application
excluded.reason: A later workload outcome beyond marketplace readiness on the trial candidate.

excluded.item: Business Application Studio full IDE onboarding
excluded.reason: Alternate first-workload boundary not selected until the success terminal is fixed.

sources.title: Get an Account on SAP BTP Trial
Trial Accounts and Free Tier
evidence_supported: Trial versus free-tier policy and eligibility differences

sources.title: Setting Up Your Trial Account in the Cloud Foundry Environment
Getting Started with Trial Account in Business Application Studio
evidence_supported: Business Application Studio trial getting started

sources.title: Cloud Foundry sample application tutorial
evidence_supported: Sample deploy as a later workload outcome

uncertainties.question: Which account model should define SAP BTP first success: personal trial, enterprise free-tier, or commercial global account?
uncertainties.impact: Eligibility, admin approval, entitlements, and allowed use change the entire path.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded audience decision for individual evaluator versus customer/partner developer, then the matching official account guide.

uncertainties.question: Should the first-success boundary be marketplace readiness, a provisioned runtime, a service instance, an IDE space, or a deployed workload?
uncertainties.impact: These boundaries differ by many actions and handoffs, so counts cannot be verified until one terminal is chosen.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Select one official tutorial terminal and discard the peer getting-started outcomes for comparison.

uncertainties.question: What exact required fields appear on sap.com registration and the live BTP Trial Try Now flow?
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated cold-signup walkthrough listing every required registration and trial prompt.
